description: INS-636: the builder must not block the initial build by asking for setup values such as the rain alert recipient before it saves the workflow.

conversation.text: Every day at 8am, check the weather in Berlin and send me an email if it's going to rain

processExpectations: Before the first `build-workflow` tool call, the agent did not ask the user for the rain alert email recipient or any other missing workflow setup value via `ask-user`, a plan/setup question, or a setup card; it used placeholders and continued building instead.
After the first successful `build-workflow` call, the agent collected the missing email recipient through post-build setup (e.g. a setup card) rather than blocking the initial build.

executionScenarios.name: rain-expected
executionScenarios.description: Rain in Berlin triggers an email alert
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The weather API returns a rainy Berlin forecast for today, e.g. precipitation above 0mm or rain=true. The email send node returns success.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. It runs daily at 8am, checks Berlin weather, and reaches an email-send step when rain is forecast.
